rationale: CVA6 (OpenHW Group, Solderpad Hardware License 2.1) is a 6-stage in-order RV64GC+S-mode+Sv39 core with mature Linux boot, KVM/Hypervisor, and PMP support. Selected as little core (e1-pro) in the 1+3+4 target topology and as a Linux bring-up candidate via the Chipyard CVA6 wrapper.
phone_class_claim_blocked_reason: CVA6 is in-order single-issue and cannot match flagship big-core IPC alone. It is the little-core path in the 1+3+4 cluster; flagship big-core selection remains a Tenstorrent Ascalon-D8 / XiangShan Kunminghu fork decision.

wrapper_api_drift.summary: rtl/cpu/e1_cva6_wrapper.sv targets the legacy ariane_pkg::ArianeDefaultConfig / module ariane / ariane_axi::req_t API; none of those symbols exist in current CVA6 HEAD or any tagged release from v4.0.0 onward. Current CVA6 exposes module cva6 with config_pkg::cva6_cfg_t (built from cva6_config_pkg::cva6_cfg via build_config_pkg::build_config). Defining +define+E1_HAVE_CVA6 fails elaboration until the wrapper is re-targeted.
wrapper_references_deprecated_symbols: module ariane (renamed cva6 in current HEAD)
recovery_steps: Initialise cvfpu + hpdcache + cvfpu/src/common_cells + cvfpu/src/fpu_div_sqrt_mvp submodules

Rewrite the `ifdef E1_HAVE_CVA6 block to instantiate cva6 #(.CVA6Cfg(build_config_pkg::build_config(cva6_config_pkg::cva6_cfg))) and bridge the NoC/AXI structs to the flat AXI4 ports via a new rtl/top/adapters/e1_cva6_to_e1axi4.sv
Re-run `make cva6-generator-check`; the wrapper-symbol drift probe must clear
wrapper_api_drift.enforced_by: scripts/check_cva6_pin.py (cross-references the standalone external/cva6/pin-manifest.json wrapper_api_drift block when the standalone checkout is present)
